steps.title: Define what the quotation must show
steps.content:
The claim: Nikhil imagines people he sees are carefree even when engaged in work. The quotation must (1) involve someone doing work or chores, and (2) show Nikhil perceiving that person as cheerful or carefree.
  • Choice A — "I see, you don't wish to stop; I don't care to stay on here either.": Nikhil talks about his own feelings, not the flower seller's daughter's mood ✗
  • Choice B — "Oh, flower gathering? That is why your feet seem so glad and your anklets jingle so merrily as you walk.": names the work (flower gathering) and attributes joy to the worker (glad feet, merry anklets) ✓
  • Choice C — "I'll pay when I grow up—before I leave to look for work out on the other side of that stream there.": Nikhil discusses his own future plans, not the daughter's mood ✗
  • Choice D — "Wish I could be out too. Then I would pick some flowers for you from the very topmost branches right out of sight.": Nikhil expresses his own wishes, not the daughter's feelings ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
Only the matching choice has Nikhil (1) identifying that the daughter is working — "flower gathering" — and (2) reading her physical demeanor as joyful — "glad" feet and "merrily" jingling anklets. That directly illustrates the claim about Nikhil imagining workers as carefree.

B is correct.
